title: 1251. What is the capital of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It is named after a British naval officer.
summary: Port Blair is the capital of the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

title: 1252. The Cellular Jail, also known as 'Kala Pani', is located in which city?
hint: It is the capital of the Union Territory.
summary: The Cellular Jail is located in Port Blair.

title: 1253. Which is the southernmost point of the territory of India?
hint: It lies in Great Nicobar Island.
summary: Indira Point is the southernmost point of the territory of India.

title: 1254. Indira Point is located on which island?
hint: It is the largest island in the Nicobar group.
summary: Indira Point is situated on Great Nicobar Island.

title: 1255. The Andaman and Nicobar Islands are separated by which channel?
hint: The channel is named after a British naval officer.
summary: The Ten Degree Channel separates the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.

title: 1256. Which is the only active volcano in India?
hint: It is located in the Andaman Sea.
summary: Barren Island is home to India's only active volcano.

title: 1257. Narcondam Island is famous for which endangered bird?
hint: It is found only on this island.
summary: Narcondam Island is home to the endemic Narcondam Hornbill.

title: 1258. Which sea surrounds the Andaman and Nicobar Islands?
hint: It lies east of the Bay of Bengal.
summary: The islands are located in the Andaman Sea and the Bay of Bengal.

title: 451. Who founded Buddhism?
hint: He was born as Siddhartha Gautama.
summary: Buddhism was founded by Gautama Buddha.

title: 452. What was the original name of Buddha before enlightenment?
hint: He was a prince of the Shakya clan.
summary: Buddha was born as Siddhartha Gautama.

title: 453. At which place was Gautama Buddha born?
hint: This site is now in Nepal.
summary: Buddha was born at Lumbini.

title: 454. Under which tree did Buddha attain enlightenment?
hint: The tree became sacred in Buddhism.
summary: Buddha attained enlightenment under the Bodhi Tree.

title: 455. Where did Buddha attain enlightenment?
hint: Located in present-day Bihar.
summary: Buddha attained enlightenment at Bodh Gaya.

title: 456. What is the meaning of the word 'Buddha'?
hint: It refers to a state of spiritual realization.
summary: Buddha means 'The Enlightened One'.

title: 551. What is the capital of Rajasthan?
hint: Known as the Pink City.
summary: Jaipur is the capital of Rajasthan.

title: 552. Which city is the capital of Maharashtra?
hint: India's financial capital.
summary: Mumbai is Maharashtra's capital.

title: 553. Which city is known as the Silicon Valley of India?
hint: Capital of Karnataka.
summary: Bengaluru is India's IT hub.

title: 554. Jaipur is situated on which river?
hint: A tributary of the Banganga.
summary: Jaipur is situated near the Dravyavati River.

title: 555. Which city is known as the Pink City?
hint: Capital of Rajasthan.
summary: Jaipur is called the Pink City.

title: 556. Which city is called the City of Lakes?
hint: Located in southern Rajasthan.
summary: Udaipur is famous as the City of Lakes.

title: 351. Which Article of the Indian Constitution guarantees Equality Before Law?
hint: It is the first article under the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 14 guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.

title: 352. Which Article abolishes Untouchability?
hint: It is part of the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

title: 353. Which Article guarantees protection of life and personal liberty?
hint: It became very important after the Maneka Gandhi case.
summary: Article 21 guarantees protection of life and personal liberty.

title: 354. Which Article is known as the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution according to Dr. B.R. Ambedkar?
hint: It provides the Right to Constitutional Remedies.
summary: Article 32 allows citizens to directly approach the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

title: 355. Which Article prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth?
hint: It comes immediately after Article 14.
summary: Article 15 prohibits discrimination by the State on specified grounds.

title: 356. Which Article guarantees equality of opportunity in public employment?
hint: Closely related to government jobs.
summary: Article 16 ensures equality of opportunity in matters of public employment.

title: 357. Which Article abolishes titles except military and academic distinctions?
hint: Prevents the State from conferring hereditary titles.
summary: Article 18 abolishes titles and promotes equality.

title: 1151. On which river is the Bhakra Dam constructed?
hint: It is one of the five rivers of Punjab.
summary: Bhakra Dam is constructed across the Sutlej River in Himachal Pradesh.

title: 1152. Hirakud Dam is built across which river?
hint: The river flows through Chhattisgarh and Odisha.
summary: Hirakud Dam is built across the Mahanadi River.

title: 1153. Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is situated on which river?
hint: It is the second-longest river in peninsular India.
summary: Nagarjuna Sagar Dam is constructed across the Krishna River.

title: 1154. Sardar Sarovar Dam is built on which river?
hint: It flows westward into the Arabian Sea.
summary: Sardar Sarovar Dam is constructed across the Narmada River.

title: 1155. Tehri Dam is constructed across which river?
hint: It is one of the headstreams of the Ganga.
summary: Tehri Dam is built across the Bhagirathi River.

title: 1156. Idukki Dam is located in which Indian state?
hint: Its capital is Thiruvananthapuram.
summary: Idukki Dam is located in Kerala.

title: 1157. Which dam is built across the Kosi River?
hint: It is located near the India–Nepal border.
summary: The Kosi Barrage is built across the Kosi River.

title: 1301. Which organization is responsible for the research and development of defence technologies in India?
hint: It was established in 1958.
summary: DRDO is India's premier defence research and development organization.

title: 1302. Which is the supreme command authority of the Indian Armed Forces?
hint: The position is held by the constitutional head of India.
summary: The President of India is the Supreme Commander of the Armed Forces.

title: 1303. Which is the oldest armed force of India?
hint: Its origins date back to the East India Company.
summary: The Indian Army is the oldest of India's armed forces.

title: 1304. The headquarters of the Indian Navy is located in:
hint: It is India's financial capital.
summary: The Naval Headquarters is in New Delhi, while The Indian Western Naval Command in Mumbai.

title: 1305. Which force is primarily responsible for guarding India's international land borders?
hint: It is one of India's Central Armed Police Forces.
summary: The Border Security Force guards most of India's international land borders.

title: 1306. Which Indian defence force celebrates its Raising Day on 15 January?
hint: Field Marshal K.M. Cariappa became its first Indian Commander-in-Chief on this date.
summary: Indian Army Day is celebrated every year on 15 January.

title: 1307. Which missile is India's first indigenously developed ballistic missile?
hint: It is named after a mythological weapon.
summary: Prithvi was India's first indigenously developed ballistic missile.

title: 501. What does the term 'Endangered Species' mean?
hint: It refers to species facing a high risk of disappearing from the wild.
summary: Endangered species face a very high risk of extinction in the near future.

options.title: Species facing a very high risk of extinction

title: 502. Which organization publishes the Red List of Threatened Species?
hint: Its full form begins with International Union...
summary: The IUCN maintains the global Red List.

title: 503. Which of the following is a Critically Endangered bird of India?
hint: It is the State Bird of Rajasthan.
summary: The Great Indian Bustard is Critically Endangered.

title: 504. Which marine mammal is India's National Aquatic Animal?
hint: It lives in the Ganga river.
summary: The Gangetic Dolphin is India's National Aquatic Animal.

title: 505. Which big cat is found only in the Gir Forest of India?
hint: It is not a tiger.
summary: The Asiatic Lion survives in Gir National Park.

title: 506. Which reptile is known for its long, narrow snout and is Critically Endangered?
hint: It is mainly found in river ecosystems.
summary: The Gharial is a Critically Endangered crocodilian.

title: 507. The Red Panda is mainly found in which region of India?
hint: Look towards the Eastern Himalayas.
summary: Red Pandas inhabit Sikkim, Arunachal Pradesh and nearby Himalayan forests.

title: 508. Which convention regulates international trade in endangered species?
hint: Its abbreviation starts with the letter 'C'.
summary: CITES regulates international trade in threatened wildlife and plants.

title: 509. Which species is protected under Project Tiger?
hint: It is India's national animal.
summary: Project Tiger was launched in 1973 for Bengal Tiger conservation.

title: 601. Which Part of the Indian Constitution contains the Fundamental Rights?
hint: It comes after the Directive Principles chapter.
summary: Fundamental Rights are contained in Part III of the Constitution.

title: 602. Fundamental Rights are provided under Articles:
hint: Starts with Article 12.
summary: Fundamental Rights are covered under Articles 12–35.

title: 603. Which Article guarantees Equality before Law?
hint: It is the first Article under the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 14 guarantees Equality before Law and Equal Protection of Laws.

title: 604. Which Article prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth?
hint: It comes immediately after Article 14.
summary: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on specified grounds.

title: 605. Equality of opportunity in public employment is guaranteed under:
hint: Think government jobs.
summary: Article 16 provides equality of opportunity in public employment.

title: 606. Which Article abolishes Untouchability?
hint: It follows Article 16.
summary: Article 17 abolishes Untouchability.

title: 607. Which Article abolishes titles except military and academic distinctions?
hint: It is the last Article under the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 18 abolishes titles.

title: 608. Which Fundamental Right includes freedom of speech and expression?
hint: It begins with Article 19.
summary: Freedom of speech and expression is guaranteed under Article 19(1)(a).

title: 609. Which of the following is NOT a freedom guaranteed under Article 19?
hint: Think about the Right to Property.
summary: The Right to Property is a legal right under Article 300A, not a Fundamental Right.

options.title: Freedom to reside anywhere in India

title: 610. Which Fundamental Right was removed by the 44th Constitutional Amendment?
hint: It is now a legal right under Article 300A.
summary: The Right to Property ceased to be a Fundamental Right after the 44th Amendment.

title: 611. Protection in respect of conviction for offences is guaranteed under which Article?
hint: It comes immediately after Article 19.
summary: Article 20 provides protection against ex post facto laws, double jeopardy and self-incrimination.
hint: Think about criminal law safeguards.

title: 612. Article 21 guarantees which Fundamental Right?
summary: Article 21 guarantees Protection of Life and Personal Liberty.
hint: It is one of the most widely interpreted Articles.

options.title: Protection of Life and Personal Liberty

title: 1. Which is the largest continent in the world?
summary: Asia is the largest continent by both area and population.

title: 2. Which is the longest river in the world?
summary: The Nile River is traditionally regarded as the longest river in the world.

title: 3. Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
summary: The Pacific Ocean covers more area than all other oceans.

title: 4. Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
summary: Mars appears reddish due to iron oxide on its surface.

title: 5. Which is the highest mountain peak in the world?
summary: Mount Everest is the highest peak above sea level.

title: 6. Which is the largest desert in the world?
summary: Antarctica is technically the largest desert due to extremely low precipitation.

title: 7. What is the capital of Australia?
summary: Canberra was selected as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

title: 1. Who is known as the 'Father of History'?
summary: Herodotus, a Greek historian, is often called the 'Father of History' for his systematic collection of historical events.

title: 2. The Indus Valley Civilization developed mainly along which river?
summary: The Indus Valley Civilization flourished along the Indus River and its tributaries.

title: 3. Who built the Taj Mahal?
summary: The Taj Mahal was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.

title: 4. Who was the first Prime Minister of independent India?
summary: Jawaharlal Nehru became the first Prime Minister of India after independence in 1947.

title: 5. Who founded the Maurya Empire?
summary: Chandragupta Maurya established the Maurya Empire with the guidance of Chanakya.

title: 6. Who wrote the Indian National Anthem?
summary: Rabindranath Tagore wrote 'Jana Gana Mana', the National Anthem of India.

title: 7. Which script was used in the Ashokan inscriptions?
summary: Ashokan inscriptions were mainly written in the Brahmi script, the earliest writing system of ancient India.

title: 8. Where did Gautama Buddha attain enlightenment?
summary: Gautama Buddha attained enlightenment at Bodh Gaya under the Bodhi tree.

title: 9. Who was the last Mughal Emperor of India?
summary: Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal Emperor, exiled after the Revolt of 1857.

title: 10. Which Indian leader is known as the 'Iron Man of India'?
summary: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel is widely known as the 'Iron Man of India' for his role in the political integration of India.

title: 1001. Which festival is known as the festival of lights?
hint: It is celebrated with lamps and fireworks.
summary: Diwali is known as the festival of lights.

title: 1002. Which festival is known as the festival of colours?
hint: It is celebrated during spring season.
summary: Holi is known as the festival of colours.

title: 1003. Pongal is mainly celebrated in which state?
hint: It is a major harvest festival of South India.
summary: Pongal is mainly celebrated in Tamil Nadu.

title: 1004. Onam is the major festival of which state?
hint: It is associated with the legend of King Mahabali.
summary: Onam is celebrated mainly in Kerala.

title: 1005. Baisakhi is mainly associated with which state?
hint: It is a harvest festival of northern India.
summary: Baisakhi is mainly celebrated in Punjab.

title: 901. Bhangra is a famous folk dance of which state?
hint: It is associated with the harvest festival Baisakhi.
summary: Bhangra is a traditional folk dance of Punjab.

title: 902. Garba is a famous folk dance of which state?
hint: It is performed during Navratri.
summary: Garba is a traditional folk dance of Gujarat.

title: 903. Ghoomar is a famous folk dance of which state?
hint: It is associated with royal traditions of western India.
summary: Ghoomar is a traditional folk dance of Rajasthan.

title: 904. Bihu dance is associated with which state?
hint: It is performed during a famous spring festival.
summary: Bihu dance is a folk dance of Assam.

title: 905. Lavani is a famous folk dance of which state?
hint: It is performed with traditional musical beats.
summary: Lavani is a folk dance of Maharashtra.

title: 1401. In which year was the first passenger train in India started?
hint: It ran between Bombay and Thane.
summary: The first passenger train in India started in 1853 between Bombay (Mumbai) and Thane.

title: 1402. The headquarters of Indian Railways is located in which city?
hint: It is the national capital of India.
summary: Railway Board headquarters is located in New Delhi.

title: 1403. Which is the largest railway zone of Indian Railways by route length?
hint: It is headquartered in Jaipur.
summary: Northern Railway is among the largest railway zones by route length.

title: 1404. Which was the first railway zone created after the reorganisation of Indian Railways?
hint: It was created in 1951.
summary: Southern Railway was the first railway zone of independent India.

title: 1405. Which Indian railway station is known as the busiest railway station in India?
hint: It is located in Maharashtra.
summary: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus is one of India's busiest railway stations.

title: 401. The Indus Valley Civilization is also known by which other name?
hint: It is named after the first site that was discovered.
summary: The Indus Valley Civilization is also known as the Harappan Civilization because Harappa was the first site discovered.
questionTags: History,Ancient History,Indus Valley Civilization,Harappan Civilization,UPSC,RPSC,SSC,Bank PO

title: 402. In which present-day country is Harappa located?
hint: It lies in Punjab province outside India.
summary: Harappa is located in present-day Pakistan.

title: 403. Who first discovered Harappa in 1921?
hint: An archaeologist working under ASI.
summary: Harappa was discovered by Daya Ram Sahni in 1921.

title: 404. Which river was closely associated with the Indus Valley Civilization?
hint: The civilization gets its name from this river.
summary: The Indus River was the principal river associated with the civilization.

title: 405. The mature phase of the Indus Valley Civilization is generally dated to which period?
hint: It falls roughly in the Bronze Age.
summary: The mature Harappan phase is generally dated between 2600 BCE and 1900 BCE.

title: 406. Which metal was NOT known to the Harappans?
hint: This metal became common much later in India.
summary: Iron was not known to the Harappans during the mature phase.

title: 851. Which is the largest lake in India by area?
hint: It is a brackish water lagoon in Odisha.
summary: Chilika Lake is the largest coastal lagoon and largest lake in India by area.

title: 852. Wular Lake is located in which state/UT?
hint: It is located in the Kashmir Valley.
summary: Wular Lake is located in Jammu and Kashmir.

title: 853. Which is the largest freshwater lake in India?
hint: It is located in Jammu and Kashmir.
summary: Wular Lake is the largest freshwater lake in India.

title: 1351. Lakshadweep is a Union Territory of India located in which sea?
hint: It is located southwest of the Indian mainland.
summary: Lakshadweep islands are located in the Arabian Sea.

title: 1352. What is the capital of Lakshadweep Union Territory?
hint: It is located on Kavaratti Island.
summary: Kavaratti is the capital and largest inhabited island of Lakshadweep.

title: 1353. Lakshadweep islands are mainly of which geological origin?
hint: They are associated with coral formations.
summary: Lakshadweep islands are coral atolls formed mainly from coral reefs.

title: 1354. Which is the largest island in the Lakshadweep group by area?
hint: It is located in the southern part of Lakshadweep.
summary: Andrott Island is the largest island of Lakshadweep by area.

title: 1355. How many islands are there in the Lakshadweep archipelago?
hint: This is the official number of islands in the Union Territory.
summary: Lakshadweep consists of 36 islands, including inhabited and uninhabited islands.

title: 1201. Nathu La Pass connects Sikkim with which neighbouring country?
hint: It shares the world's longest land border with India.

title: 1202. Zoji La Pass connects Ladakh with which region?
hint: It is famous for its beautiful valley.

title: 1203. Rohtang Pass is located in which Indian state?
hint: Its capital is Shimla.

title: 1204. Khardung La Pass is located in which Union Territory?
hint: It is home to Pangong Tso.
summary: Khardung La Pass is located in Ladakh.

title: 1205. Which mountain pass connects Manali with Leh?
hint: It lies on the Manali–Leh Highway.

title: 1206. Jelep La Pass is located on the border of India and which country?
hint: It is east of Nepal.
summary: Jelep La Pass lies on the India–China border.

title: 1207. Bomdi La Pass is located in which state?
hint: Its capital is Itanagar.
summary: Bomdi La Pass is located in Arunachal Pradesh.

title: 1208. Shipki La Pass connects Himachal Pradesh with which neighbouring country?
hint: It is India's largest neighbouring country.
summary: Shipki La Pass connects Himachal Pradesh with China.

title: 701. Which is the oldest mountain range in India?

title: 702. The Aravalli Range extends from Gujarat to which state/UT?
hint: Its capital is New Delhi.

title: 703. Which is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range?
hint: It is located near Mount Abu.
summary: Guru Shikhar is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range.

title: 651. Which is the highest mountain peak in the world?
hint: It lies on the Nepal–China border.
summary: Mount Everest is the highest mountain peak in the world at 8,848.86 m.

title: 652. Mount Everest belongs to which mountain range?
hint: The world's youngest and highest fold mountains.
summary: Mount Everest is part of the Himalayas.

title: 653. Which is the second-highest mountain in the world?
hint: It lies in the Karakoram Range.

title: 654. K2 is located in which mountain range?

title: 655. Which is the highest peak in North America?
hint: It was formerly known as Mount McKinley.
summary: Denali is the highest mountain in North America.

title: 151. Which is the oldest National Park in India?
hint: It was originally called Hailey National Park.

title: 152. Gir National Park is famous for conserving which animal?
hint: It is the only wild population of this species in Asia.
summary: Gir National Park in Gujarat is the last natural habitat of the Asiatic Lion.

title: 153. Kaziranga National Park is located in which state?
hint: The Brahmaputra River flows through this state.

hint: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Assam.
summary: Kaziranga National Park is globally known for the Indian one-horned rhinoceros.

title: 155. Sundarbans National Park is known for which ecosystem?
hint: Trees here grow in salty coastal water.

title: 156. Which National Park is located in Rajasthan and famous for tiger sightings?
hint: It contains a historic fort within the park.

title: 157. Keoladeo National Park is especially famous for which group of animals?
hint: Bird watchers from around the world visit here.

title: 51. Who is known as the Father of the Indian Constitution?
hint: He was the Chairman of the Drafting Committee.
summary: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar played the leading role in drafting the Constitution of India.

title: 52. How many Fundamental Rights are currently guaranteed by the Constitution of India?
hint: Originally there were seven; one was later removed.
summary: At present, there are six Fundamental Rights in the Constitution.

title: 53. What is the supreme law of India?
hint: All laws must conform to it.
summary: The Constitution is the highest legal authority in India.

title: 54. Who is the constitutional head of the Union Government?
hint: This office is elected indirectly.
summary: The President is the constitutional head of India.

title: 55. Which part of the Constitution contains Fundamental Rights?
hint: It comes immediately before the Directive Principles.
summary: Part III of the Constitution contains Fundamental Rights.

title: 56. Which Article abolishes untouchability?
hint: It is one of the shortest but most important Fundamental Rights articles.
summary: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice.

title: 801. Which is the longest river in India?
hint: It flows through northern India and Bangladesh.
summary: The Ganga is the longest river flowing entirely within India.

title: 802. The source of the Ganga River is which glacier?
hint: It is located in Uttarakhand.
summary: The Ganga originates from the Gangotri Glacier as the Bhagirathi River.

title: 803. The Ganga is formed at Devprayag by the confluence of which two rivers?
hint: One originates from Gangotri Glacier.
summary: The Ganga is formed by the meeting of Bhagirathi and Alaknanda at Devprayag.

title: 804. Which is the largest tributary of the Ganga River?
hint: It flows through Delhi and Uttar Pradesh.
summary: The Yamuna is the largest tributary of the Ganga.

title: 805. The Yamuna River originates from which glacier?
hint: It is located in Uttarakhand.
summary: The Yamuna originates from the Yamunotri Glacier.

title: 806. Which river is known as the 'Dakshin Ganga'?
hint: It is the largest peninsular river of India.
summary: The Godavari River is known as Dakshin Ganga or Ganga of the South.

title: 807. Which is the longest peninsular river of India?
hint: It flows into the Bay of Bengal.

title: 751. Which is the longest river in the world?
hint: It flows through northeastern Africa.
summary: The Nile is generally regarded as the longest river in the world.

title: 752. Which river has the largest drainage basin in the world?
hint: It flows through Brazil.
summary: The Amazon River has the world's largest drainage basin.

title: 753. Which river has the greatest volume of water discharge in the world?
hint: It empties into the Atlantic Ocean.
summary: The Amazon River has the highest water discharge of any river in the world.

title: 754. The Amazon River flows through which continent?
hint: Brazil occupies a major part of this continent.
summary: The Amazon River flows through South America.

title: 1051. Which is the largest state of India by area?
hint: It is located in northwestern India.
summary: Rajasthan is the largest state in India by area.

title: 1052. Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
hint: It lies on the western coast.
summary: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states.

title: 1053. Which is the smallest state of India by area?
hint: It is famous for its beaches.
summary: Goa is the smallest state in India by area.

title: 1054. Which state is known as the 'Spice Garden of India'?
hint: It is located on the Malabar Coast.
summary: Kerala is known as the Spice Garden of India.

title: 1055. Which state shares the longest international border with Bangladesh?
hint: Its capital is Agartala.
summary: Tripura shares the longest border with Bangladesh among Indian states.

title: 101. Project Tiger was launched in which year?
hint: It was launched during the early 1970s to protect India's declining tiger population.
summary: Project Tiger was launched by the Government of India in 1973 to conserve tigers and their habitats.

title: 102. Which animal is primarily protected under Project Tiger?
hint: It is India's national animal.
summary: Project Tiger focuses on the conservation of Bengal Tigers.

title: 103. Which state is home to the Ranthambore Tiger Reserve?
hint: This state is famous for its deserts and forts.

title: 104. Which Tiger Reserve is located in Uttarakhand and was India's first national park?
hint: It was formerly known as Hailey National Park.

title: 105. Which state has the Bandipur Tiger Reserve?
hint: Its capital is Bengaluru.

title: 106. Which Tiger Reserve is famous for inspiring Rudyard Kipling's Jungle Book?

title: 301. Which Indian monument is known as the 'Symbol of Love' and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site?

title: 302. In which city is the Taj Mahal located?
hint: The city lies on the banks of the Yamuna River.

title: 303. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site in Delhi is the tallest brick minaret in India?

title: 304. Which UNESCO site in Delhi served as the residence of Mughal emperors?

title: 305. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is known as the precursor to the Taj Mahal?

title: 306. Which UNESCO World Heritage Site is famous for rock-cut Buddhist caves in Maharashtra?

title: 307. Which UNESCO site contains Hindu, Buddhist and Jain cave monuments at one location?

title: 251. Which Wildlife Sanctuary in Rajasthan is famous for blackbucks?

title: 252. Tal Chhapar Wildlife Sanctuary is famous for which animal?
hint: A graceful antelope found in grasslands.

title: 253. Sultanpur Wildlife Sanctuary was upgraded into a National Park in which state?
hint: The state surrounds Delhi on three sides.
summary: Sultanpur is located in Haryana and later became Sultanpur National Park.

title: 254. Which Wildlife Sanctuary in Delhi protects part of the Aravalli hills?
hint: It shares its name with a nearby village.
summary: Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary protects the Northern Aravalli ecosystem.

title: 255. Which Wildlife Sanctuary in Uttar Pradesh is famous for migratory birds?
hint: Located near Noida.
summary: Okhla Bird Sanctuary attracts numerous migratory bird species.

title: 256. National Chambal Sanctuary is known for conserving which critically endangered reptile?
hint: It has a very long narrow snout.
summary: National Chambal Sanctuary is India's most important habitat for the gharial.
